questions.question: Explain why component-wise processing in RGB space might introduce color artifacts.
keyPoints: Processing channels independently disrupts color relationships
Can create new colors not in original image through averaging
May cause color bleeding across edges
Does not preserve color vector integrity

questions.question: Compare and contrast component-wise smoothing versus vector median filtering for color images, discussing their applications, advantages, and limitations.
keyPoints: Component-wise: simpler, faster, but may cause artifacts
Vector median: preserves edges, reduces impulse noise, but computationally expensive
Component-wise suitable for Gaussian noise, mild smoothing
Vector median better for impulse noise, important edges
Trade-off between computational efficiency and result quality
